rationale: The cited paper appears only as one item in a grouped list of dynamic discrete choice applications in the prior-literature section (e.g., alongside Ching et al. 2012, Dube et al. 2012, Rust 1987). The citing paper does not discuss this specific drug-diffusion study's findings, assumptions, or mechanisms, and it does not use it directly for hypothesis development, model specification, or interpretation. The substantive conceptual and modeling foundation is attributed instead to Gabaix et al. (2006) and eye-tracking/search papers.
